arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven customer help chatbot using C# / Blazor Server that answers questions from internal product PDF documentation, backed by PostgreSQL for data storage.

## Approach: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
Use a RAG pattern: extract text from PDFs in a local `Docs/` folder, chunk it, generate embeddings, store them in Postgres (via pgvector), and retrieve relevant chunks at query time to feed as context to an LLM.

---

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌───────────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │───▶│  ChatService   │───▶│  OpenAI / Azure   │
│  (Server)    │    │                │    │  (LLM + Embed)    │
└──────────────┘    └───────┬────────┘    └───────────────────┘
                            │
                    ┌───────▼────────┐
                    │ DocumentService│──▶ Docs/ folder (PDFs)
                    │ (ingest + RAG) │
                    └───────┬────────┘
                            │
                    ┌───────▼────────┐
                    │  PostgreSQL    │
                    │  + pgvector    │
                    └────────────────┘
```

## Key Technology Choices
| Concern | Choice |
|---|---|
| Framework | Blazor Server (.NET 10) |
| LLM + Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI-compatible provider |
| PDF text extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Vector storage | PostgreSQL + pgvector extension (via Npgsql + Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL) |
| ORM | Entity Framework Core (Npgsql provider) |
| Embedding dimensions | 1536 (text-embedding-ada-002 default) |

## Todos

### 1. Add NuGet dependencies
Add packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` — unified AI abstractions + OpenAI provider
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` — PDF text extraction
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` — EF Core Postgres provider
- `Pgvector` + `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` — pgvector support for EF Core

### 2. Set up data model & EF Core DbContext
Create `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` with tables:
- **DocumentChunks** — `Id`, `FileName`, `ChunkIndex`, `Content`, `Embedding` (vector)
- **ChatSessions** — `Id`, `CreatedAt`
- **ChatMessages** — `Id`, `SessionId`, `Role`, `Content`, `Timestamp`

### 3. Create document ingestion service
`Services/DocumentService.cs`:
- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into ~500-token chunks (with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Expose a method to run ingestion on app startup (or on-demand)

### 4. Create chat service
`Services/ChatService.cs`:
- Accept a user question + session ID
- Generate an embedding for the question
- Perform vector similarity search against `DocumentChunks` (cosine distance, top-5)
- Build a system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call the LLM via `IChatClient` and stream the response
- Persist messages to `ChatMessages`

### 5. Build Blazor UI
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — main chat page with:
  - Message list (user & assistant bubbles)
  - Input box + send button
  - New-session button
  - Auto-scroll to latest message
- `Components/App.razor`, `Components/Routes.razor`, `Components/_Imports.razor` — Blazor boilerplate

### 6. Configure Program.cs
- Register services (EF Core, ChatService, DocumentService, AI clients)
- Read config from `appsettings.json` (connection string, OpenAI key, model names)
- Run EF migrations on startup (ensure DB + pgvector extension exist)
- Trigger document ingestion on startup
- Map Blazor endpoints

### 7. Add configuration
`appsettings.json`:
- `ConnectionStrings:DefaultConnection`
- `OpenAI:ApiKey`, `OpenAI:ChatModel`, `OpenAI:EmbeddingModel`
- `Documents:Path` (default `Docs`)

### 8. Add EF Core migration
Generate initial migration for the schema.

### 9. Validate
- Start PostgreSQL, ensure pgvector extension is available
- `dotnet build` to verify compilation
- Smoke-test the app with `dotnet run`

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder currently has a placeholder `product-guide.pdf`. The ingestion pipeline will handle real PDFs when they are added.
- An OpenAI API key (or Azure OpenAI endpoint) is required at runtime. The app will read it from configuration / environment variables.
- pgvector extension must be installed in the Postgres instance (`CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;`). The app will attempt this automatically via EF migration.
- Streaming responses to the Blazor UI will use `IAsyncEnumerable` for a smooth typing effect.

- **ChatMessages** — `Id`, `SessionId`, `Role`, `Content`, `Timestamp`

### 3. Create document ingestion service
`Services/DocumentService.cs`:
- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into ~500-token chunks (with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Expose a method to run ingestion on app startup (or on-demand)

### 4. Create chat service
`Services/ChatService.cs`:
- Accept a user question + session ID
- Generate an embedding for the question
- Perform vector similarity search against `DocumentChunks` (cosine distance, top-5)
- Build a system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call the LLM via `IChatClient` and stream the response
- Persist messages to `ChatMessages`

### 5. Build Blazor UI
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — main chat page with:
  - Message list (user & assistant bubbles)
  - Input box + send button
  - New-session button
  - Auto-scroll to latest message
- `Components/App.razor`, `Components/Routes.razor`, `Components/_Imports.razor` — Blazor boilerplate

### 6. Configure Program.cs
- Register services (EF Core, ChatService, DocumentService, AI clients)
- Read config from `appsettings.json` (connection string, OpenAI key, model names)
- Run EF migrations on startup (ensure DB + pgvector extension exist)
- Trigger document ingestion on startup
- Map Blazor endpoints

### 7. Add configuration
`appsettings.json`:
- `ConnectionStrings:DefaultConnection`
- `OpenAI:ApiKey`, `OpenAI:ChatModel`, `OpenAI:EmbeddingModel`
- `Documents:Path` (default `Docs`)

### 8. Add EF Core migration
Generate initial migration for the schema.

### 9. Validate
- Start PostgreSQL, ensure pgvector extension is available
- `dotnet build` to verify compilation
- Smoke-test the app with `dotnet run`

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder currently has a placeholder `product-guide.pdf`. The ingestion pipeline will handle real PDFs when they are added.
- An OpenAI API key (or Azure OpenAI endpoint) is required at runtime. The app will read it from configuration / environment variables.
- pgvector extension must be installed in the Postgres instance (`CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;`). The app will attempt this automatically via EF migration.
- Streaming responses to the Blazor UI will use `IAsyncEnumerable` for a smooth typing effect.

Extract text from PDFs in the `Docs/` folder, chunk it, generate embeddings, store them in Postgres (pgvector), and retrieve relevant chunks at query time as context for the LLM.

---

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌───────────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │───▶│  ChatService   │───▶│  OpenAI / Azure   │

                            │
                    ┌───────▼────────┐
                    │  PostgreSQL    │
                    │  + pgvector    │
                    └────────────────┘
```

| Concern | Choice |
|---|---|
| Framework | Blazor Server (.NET 10) |
| LLM + Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI-compatible provider |

| Vector storage | PostgreSQL + pgvector extension |
| ORM | Entity Framework Core (Npgsql provider) |

Generate initial migration.

Extract text from PDFs in the `Docs/` folder, chunk it, generate embeddings, store them in Postgres (pgvector), and retrieve relevant chunks at query time as context for the LLM.

---

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐    ┌───────────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │───▶│  ChatService   │───▶│  OpenAI / Azure   │
│  (Server)    │    │                │    │  (LLM + Embed)    │
└──────────────┘    └───────┬────────┘    └───────────────────┘
                            │
                    ┌───────▼────────┐
                    │ DocumentService│──▶ Docs/ folder (PDFs)
                    │ (ingest + RAG) │
                    └───────┬────────┘
                            │
                    ┌───────▼────────┐
                    │  PostgreSQL    │
                    │  + pgvector    │
                    └────────────────┘
```

## Key Technology Choices
| Concern | Choice |
|---|---|
| Framework | Blazor Server (.NET 10) |
| LLM + Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI-compatible provider |
| PDF text extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Vector storage | PostgreSQL + pgvector extension |
| ORM | Entity Framework Core (Npgsql provider) |
| Embedding dimensions | 1536 (text-embedding-ada-002) |

## Todos

### 1. Add NuGet dependencies
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` — AI abstractions + OpenAI provider
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` — PDF text extraction
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` — EF Core Postgres provider
- `Pgvector` + `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` — pgvector support

### 2. Data model & EF Core DbContext
`Data/ChatDbContext.cs` with:
- **DocumentChunks** — Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (vector)
- **ChatSessions** — Id, CreatedAt
- **ChatMessages** — Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp

### 3. Document ingestion service
`Services/DocumentService.cs` — scan Docs/, extract PDF text, chunk (~500 tokens w/ overlap), embed, upsert to DB.

### 4. Chat service
`Services/ChatService.cs` — embed question, vector search (top-5 cosine), build RAG prompt, call LLM with streaming, persist messages.

### 5. Blazor UI
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat page with message list, input, send, new-session
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell with CSS
- `Components/App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor` — boilerplate

### 6. Program.cs wiring
Register EF Core, AI clients, services; run migrations + ingestion on startup; map Blazor.

### 7. Configuration
`appsettings.json` — connection string, OpenAI key/models, Docs path.

### 8. EF Core migration
Generate initial migration.

### 9. Validate
`dotnet build`, start Postgres, smoke-test.

## Notes
- Placeholder PDF in `Docs/`; real PDFs will be ingested when added.
- OpenAI API key required at runtime (config or env var).
- pgvector extension created automatically via migration.
- Streaming responses via `IAsyncEnumerable` for typing effect.
